info.title: Renewable Energy Executive Dashboard API
info.description:
FastAPI backend serving renewable energy research data for the executive dashboard. All data is derived from the March 2026 research phase covering the North American construction industry.

**Research confidence levels:**
- `HIGH` – multiple corroborating sources
- `MEDIUM` – single reliable source or partial data
- `LOW` – inference from related data or limited sourcing

get.description: Returns the current health status of the API and its database connection.

get.summary: Adoption rates and market size data
get.description:
Returns global and North American green building market size data, electric construction equipment market data, IRA incentive totals, adoption rates by firm size, time-series projections, and key market drivers and barriers.

**Key data points:**
- Global green building market: $618.58B (2025) → $1,374.23B (2034), CAGR 9.29%
- North America: ~35% of global market ($153.87B in 2025)
- Electric construction equipment: $13.63–15.77B (2025), projected $34.72B by 2030 (CAGR 20.5%)
- Mid-size firms ($50M–$500M): 40–55% have adopted some renewable energy strategy

Returns USGBC Top 10 States for LEED (2024 and 2025), country-level data for USA, Canada, and Mexico, and key state/provincial policy drivers.

**Key data points:**
- US: 56M gross sq meters LEED-certified space (global leader)
- Canada: 10.07M sq meters, 2nd globally; Canada Green Buildings Strategy 2024
- Mexico: 2.27M sq meters, 6th globally; first LEED v5 O+M and industrial certifications

get.summary: Technology catalog with feasibility scores and ROI
get.description:
Returns all tracked renewable energy and green building technologies with feasibility scores (0–10), ROI metrics, payback estimates, and adoption data.

**Technologies covered:**
- Electric Construction Equipment (excavators, cranes, loaders)
- Solar PV (on-site, BIPV)
- Green Building Systems (HVAC, LED, stormwater)
- Battery Storage (construction site and building-integrated)
- Green Hydrogen
- Mass Timber / CLT
- AI Energy Management Systems

Returns a full total cost of ownership (TCO) analysis comparing the specified renewable/green technology against its conventional equivalent, including multi-scenario analysis over 10–20 year horizons.

**Technology IDs:**
- `electric-excavator`
- `solar-pv`
- `green-building-systems`
- `battery-storage`
- `green-hydrogen`
- `mass-timber`
- `ai-energy-management`

Returns payback period analysis for all tracked technologies, with and without regulatory incentives.

**Key data points:**
- Range: 5–12 years depending on technology and incentive availability
- Solar PV: 7–12 years base; 5–8 years with IRA credits
- Electric equipment: 6–10 years base; 4–7 years with IRA credits
- LEED management software: 3–6 months

Returns three multi-year ROI projection scenarios for a representative mid-size construction firm ($150M revenue) adopting a portfolio of green technologies over a 10-year horizon.

**Scenarios:**
- **Conservative**: Minimum viable adoption (LEED AP credentialing + management software); no major capex
- **Moderate**: Full sustainability program with on-site solar demonstration and electric equipment pilot
- **Aggressive**: Comprehensive transformation including mass timber capability, full fleet electrification, and SBTi commitment

get.summary: Regulatory incentives and their financial impact
get.description:
Returns all tracked federal, state, and provincial incentive programs relevant to green building and renewable energy adoption in construction.

**Key programs:**
- IRA Section 48 Investment Tax Credit (30%+ for solar, storage)
- IRA Section 45 Production Tax Credit
- Section 179D Energy-Efficient Commercial Buildings Deduction (expanded by IRA)
- Federal LEED Gold mandate (GSA – market access, not financial)
- State programs: CA, NY, CO, MA, WA, IL
- Canada: Greener Homes Initiative, Clean Investment Tax Credit

**Note:** Political risk flagged where OBBBA may modify credits.

Returns competitive benchmarking profiles for six leading construction firms that have implemented renewable energy and sustainability strategies, with ROI metrics and lessons applicable to mid-size firms.

**Firms profiled:**
- **Mortenson** (Minneapolis) – $5.5B; #1 ENR Battery Storage; 40 GW wind, 12+ GW solar
- **Gilbane** (Providence) – $7.3B; #7 ENR Green 2024; regenerative construction; SBTi Net Zero 2040
- **Suffolk** (Boston) – $5B+; $2.6B annual green revenue; 43M+ SF decarbonization
- **Swinerton** (San Francisco) – $4.5B; Timberlab mass timber; 200 MW solar
- **Hensel Phelps** (Greeley) – $7.4B; $192M net-zero microgrid; $1M+ LEED savings
- **Brasfield & Gorrie** (Birmingham) – $6.5B; #1 ENR Texas & Southeast; LEED upselling

Returns data on the correlation between green credentials/certifications and contract win rates, including certification market data, bid win rate evidence, contract premiums, and client preference analysis.

**Key data points:**
- LEED referenced in 71% of specs for projects >$50M
- 35+ US states mandate LEED or equivalent for public buildings
- GSA requires LEED Gold for new federal buildings
- Sustainability criteria worth 10–25% of evaluation points in government RFPs
- LEED O+M certifications grew 79% (2019–2024)
- Green-certified firms effectively excluded from majority of large-project bids without credentials
- 25–35% higher contract win rates on green-specified projects

field.description: Field name if this is a validation error

ConfidenceLevel.description: Research data confidence level based on source quality and corroboration

example.Higher Education: LEED Silver or Gold as baseline for campus construction
example.Corporate ESG: LEED Gold/Platinum for headquarters; ESG reporting drives demand
